info.name: LoneWolf API
info.description:
Full API for the LoneWolf event platform. Each request has a description of what it does, and saved example responses (success + relevant error cases) so you can see the expected result without running the request yourself.

Workflow: Register -> Login (auto-saves {{token}} and {{userId}}) -> Create Event (auto-saves {{eventId}}) -> everything else.

request.description: Creates a new Registered User. Hashes the password with bcrypt before storing it. Fails with 400 if the email is already in use, or if name/email/password fail validation (e.g. password under 6 characters).

originalRequest.description: Creates a new Registered User. Hashes the password with bcrypt before storing it. Fails with 400 if the email is already in use, or if name/email/password fail validation (e.g. password under 6 characters).

Creates a new Registered User. Hashes the password with bcrypt before storing it. Fails with 400 if the email is already in use, or if name/email/password fail validation (e.g. password under 6 characters).

Creates a new Registered User. Hashes the password with bcrypt before storing it. Fails with 400 if the email is already in use, or if name/email/password fail validation (e.g. password under 6 characters).

request.description: Authenticates a user and returns a signed JWT (valid 7 days). On success, this request's test script automatically saves the token into {{token}} and the user id into {{userId}} for use by every other protected request in this collection.

originalRequest.description: Authenticates a user and returns a signed JWT (valid 7 days). On success, this request's test script automatically saves the token into {{token}} and the user id into {{userId}} for use by every other protected request in this collection.

Authenticates a user and returns a signed JWT (valid 7 days). On success, this request's test script automatically saves the token into {{token}} and the user id into {{userId}} for use by every other protected request in this collection.

request.description: Returns the profile of whoever owns the Bearer token (password field excluded). Protected - requires a valid token.

originalRequest.description: Returns the profile of whoever owns the Bearer token (password field excluded). Protected - requires a valid token.
Returns the profile of whoever owns the Bearer token (password field excluded). Protected - requires a valid token.

request.description: Lists every registered user (password excluded). Protected: the caller's JWT must have role 'admin', enforced by the adminOnly middleware.

originalRequest.description: Lists every registered user (password excluded). Protected: the caller's JWT must have role 'admin', enforced by the adminOnly middleware.
Lists every registered user (password excluded). Protected: the caller's JWT must have role 'admin', enforced by the adminOnly middleware.

request.description: Sets isBanned = true on the target user and writes a USER_BANNED entry to the audit log (visible via GET /api/admin/audit). Protected: admin only.

originalRequest.description: Sets isBanned = true on the target user and writes a USER_BANNED entry to the audit log (visible via GET /api/admin/audit). Protected: admin only.
Sets isBanned = true on the target user and writes a USER_BANNED entry to the audit log (visible via GET /api/admin/audit). Protected: admin only.

request.description: Lists events. Public - no auth required. Supports optional query filters: ?category=, ?start=, ?end= (ISO dates), filtering on the startTime field.

originalRequest.description: Lists events. Public - no auth required. Supports optional query filters: ?category=, ?start=, ?end= (ISO dates), filtering on the startTime field.

request.description: Finds events within `radius` km of (lat, lng) using the Haversine formula, limited to events that haven't ended yet. Public. Defaults radius to 10km if omitted. Returns 400 if lat/lng are missing or not numbers.

originalRequest.description: Finds events within `radius` km of (lat, lng) using the Haversine formula, limited to events that haven't ended yet. Public. Defaults radius to 10km if omitted. Returns 400 if lat/lng are missing or not numbers.

response.name: 400 - Missing coordinates

originalRequest.description: Finds events within `radius` km of (lat, lng) using the Haversine formula, limited to events that haven't ended yet. Public. Defaults radius to 10km if omitted. Returns 400 if lat/lng are missing or not numbers.

request.description: Fetches a single event by id. Public. Returns 404 if the id doesn't exist, 400 if the id isn't a valid Mongo ObjectId.

originalRequest.description: Fetches a single event by id. Public. Returns 404 if the id doesn't exist, 400 if the id isn't a valid Mongo ObjectId.
Fetches a single event by id. Public. Returns 404 if the id doesn't exist, 400 if the id isn't a valid Mongo ObjectId.

request.description: Publishes a new event. Protected - hostId is taken from the verified JWT, never from the request body. On success, this request's test script saves the new event's id into {{eventId}}. Returns 400 on validation failure (bad dates, endTime before startTime, out-of-range coordinates).

originalRequest.description: Publishes a new event. Protected - hostId is taken from the verified JWT, never from the request body. On success, this request's test script saves the new event's id into {{eventId}}. Returns 400 on validation failure (bad dates, endTime before startTime, out-of-range coordinates).

Publishes a new event. Protected - hostId is taken from the verified JWT, never from the request body. On success, this request's test script saves the new event's id into {{eventId}}. Returns 400 on validation failure (bad dates, endTime before startTime, out-of-range coordinates).

request.description: Partially updates an event. Protected - only the event's own host (hostId matches the caller's id) may update it. Any field omitted from the body is left unchanged.

originalRequest.description: Partially updates an event. Protected - only the event's own host (hostId matches the caller's id) may update it. Any field omitted from the body is left unchanged.

response.name: 403 - Not the host

originalRequest.description: Partially updates an event. Protected - only the event's own host (hostId matches the caller's id) may update it. Any field omitted from the body is left unchanged.

request.description: Deletes an event. Protected - allowed for the event's host, or for any admin. Writes an EVENT_DELETED entry to the audit log on success.

originalRequest.description: Deletes an event. Protected - allowed for the event's host, or for any admin. Writes an EVENT_DELETED entry to the audit log on success.

response.name: 403 - Not authorized

originalRequest.description: Deletes an event. Protected - allowed for the event's host, or for any admin. Writes an EVENT_DELETED entry to the audit log on success.

request.description: Reserves a spot for the caller in the event's participants list. Protected. Capacity and duplicate-join checks happen in one atomic findOneAndUpdate, so two simultaneous join requests can't both slip past a 'still has room' check. Returns 400 if already joined or if the event is full.

originalRequest.description: Reserves a spot for the caller in the event's participants list. Protected. Capacity and duplicate-join checks happen in one atomic findOneAndUpdate, so two simultaneous join requests can't both slip past a 'still has room' check. Returns 400 if already joined or if the event is full.

Reserves a spot for the caller in the event's participants list. Protected. Capacity and duplicate-join checks happen in one atomic findOneAndUpdate, so two simultaneous join requests can't both slip past a 'still has room' check. Returns 400 if already joined or if the event is full.

request.description: Removes the caller from the event's participants list. Protected. Safe to call even if the user wasn't a participant - $pull is a no-op in that case.

originalRequest.description: Removes the caller from the event's participants list. Protected. Safe to call even if the user wasn't a participant - $pull is a no-op in that case.
Removes the caller from the event's participants list. Protected. Safe to call even if the user wasn't a participant - $pull is a no-op in that case.

request.description: Generates a downloadable .ics file for the event (Content-Type: text/calendar) so it can be imported into Google/Apple/Outlook calendars. Public.

response.name: 200 - OK (.ics file)

originalRequest.description: Generates a downloadable .ics file for the event (Content-Type: text/calendar) so it can be imported into Google/Apple/Outlook calendars. Public.

response.body:
BEGIN:VCALENDAR
VERSION:2.0
PRODID:-//LoneWolf//Events//EN
BEGIN:VEVENT
UID:665f2a2b3c4d5e6f7a8b9c0e@lonewolf
DTSTAMP:20260620T180000Z
DTSTART:20260701T180000Z
DTEND:20260701T210000Z
SUMMARY:Board Game Night
END:VEVENT
END:VCALENDAR

originalRequest.description: Generates a downloadable .ics file for the event (Content-Type: text/calendar) so it can be imported into Google/Apple/Outlook calendars. Public.

request.description: Flags an event for moderation. Protected - any registered user can report an event they think is inappropriate. On success, saves the new report's id into {{reportId}}.

originalRequest.description: Flags an event for moderation. Protected - any registered user can report an event they think is inappropriate. On success, saves the new report's id into {{reportId}}.
Flags an event for moderation. Protected - any registered user can report an event they think is inappropriate. On success, saves the new report's id into {{reportId}}.

request.description: Lists every moderation report, with the reporter's name/email and the reported event's title populated. Protected: admin only.

response.name: 200 - OK

originalRequest.description: Lists every moderation report, with the reporter's name/email and the reported event's title populated. Protected: admin only.
Lists every moderation report, with the reporter's name/email and the reported event's title populated. Protected: admin only.

request.description: Marks a report as resolved and records which admin resolved it. Protected: admin only.

originalRequest.description: Marks a report as resolved and records which admin resolved it. Protected: admin only.
Marks a report as resolved and records which admin resolved it. Protected: admin only.

request.description: Leaves a 1-5 rating and optional comment for an event. Protected. A unique index on (eventId, userId) means a user can only leave feedback once per event - a second attempt returns 409.

originalRequest.description: Leaves a 1-5 rating and optional comment for an event. Protected. A unique index on (eventId, userId) means a user can only leave feedback once per event - a second attempt returns 409.

response.name: 409 - Already submitted

originalRequest.description: Leaves a 1-5 rating and optional comment for an event. Protected. A unique index on (eventId, userId) means a user can only leave feedback once per event - a second attempt returns 409.

request.description: Lists all feedback for an event, newest first, with the author's name populated. Public.

originalRequest.description: Lists all feedback for an event, newest first, with the author's name populated. Public.
Lists all feedback for an event, newest first, with the author's name populated. Public.

request.description: Lists the caller's own notifications, newest first. Protected - userId is taken from the JWT, so a user only ever sees their own.

response.name: 200 - OK

originalRequest.description: Lists the caller's own notifications, newest first. Protected - userId is taken from the JWT, so a user only ever sees their own.

request.description: Sets read = true on one notification. Protected, with an ownership check - returns 403 if the notification belongs to a different user than the caller.

originalRequest.description: Sets read = true on one notification. Protected, with an ownership check - returns 403 if the notification belongs to a different user than the caller.

response.name: 403 - Not your notification

originalRequest.description: Sets read = true on one notification. Protected, with an ownership check - returns 403 if the notification belongs to a different user than the caller.

request.description: Returns the full activity log (event deletions, user bans, etc.) with the acting admin's name/email populated. Protected: admin only.

originalRequest.description: Returns the full activity log (event deletions, user bans, etc.) with the acting admin's name/email populated. Protected: admin only.
Returns the full activity log (event deletions, user bans, etc.) with the acting admin's name/email populated. Protected: admin only.
